metadata.name: vcap
metadata.summary: A library for creating OpenVisionCapsules in Python

functions.name: vcap.deprecation.deprecated
documentation:
Mark a function as deprecated
\:param message: Extra details to be added to the warning. For example,
the details may point users to a replacement method.
\:param remove_in: The version when the decorated method will be removed.
The default is None, specifying that the function is not currently planned
to be removed.
\:param current_version: Defaults to the vcaps current version.

functions.name: vcap.testing.thread_validation.verify_all_threads_closed
documentation:
A convenient function to throw an error if all threads are not closed.

```
param allowable_threads

A list of regular expressions that match to
thread names that are allowed to stay open
```

functions.name: vcap.testing.capsule_loading.load_capsule_with_one_device
documentation:
Load the capsule, but patch out the DeviceMapper so that it never returns
multiple devices.

Essentially, this disables capsules from loading backends onto multiple
devices, for example GPUs. This is for the purpose of speeding up test
setup and teardown.

classes.name: vcap.batch_executor._Request

class_variables.name: vcap.batch_executor._Request.future
documentation: The Future object for the BatchExecutor to return the output

class_variables.name: vcap.batch_executor._Request.input_data
documentation: A unit of input data expected by the batch_fn.

Used by BatchExecutor to keep track of requests and their respective
future objects.

classes.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor

methods.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new BatchExecutor

```
param batch_fn

A function that takes in a list of inputs and iterates
```

the outputs in the same order as the inputs.
\:param max_batch_size: The maximum length of list to feed to batch_fn
\:param num_workers: How many workers should be calling batch_fn

methods.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor.submit
documentation: Submits a job and returns a Future that will be fulfilled later.

methods.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor._on_requests_ready
documentation:
Push inputs through the given prediction backend

```
param batch

A list of requests to work on
```

methods.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor._get_next_batch
documentation:
A helper function to help make the main thread loop more readable
:returns: A non-empty list of collected items, or None if the worker is
          no longer running (i.e. self._continue == False)

methods.name: vcap.batch_executor.BatchExecutor.close
documentation: Stop the BatchExecutor gracefully.

Feeds jobs into batch_fn in batches, returns results through Futures.

This class simplifies centralizing work from a multitude of sources and
running that work in a batched predict function, then returning that
work to the respective Futures.

functions.name: vcap.caching.cache
documentation:
Caches the result of a method call. Caches are specific to the instance
of the object that this method is for.

functions.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation.make_detection_node
documentation:
Creates a fake detection node that describes the given node description.
\:param frame_shape: The shape of the frame in (height, width, channels)
\:param node_description: The description that the returned node must

> adhere to

```
return

A fake detection node that adheres to this description
```

functions.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation.make_capsule_options
documentation:
Create a random set of options that are valid within the specs that the
capsule options allow

functions.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation._run_inference_on_images
documentation: Run inference on a list of images

functions.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation._test_capsule_input_output
documentation:
Creates necessary resources to load a capsule, then runs the
necessary batches to test the capsule.

functions.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation.perform_capsule_tests
documentation:
This tests several many things:
1) The capsule can handle batches of images
2) The capsule can handle different capsule option combinations
3) The capsule handles the input type that it specifies
4) The capsule does not keep references to itself, thus causing it not to
   be garbage collected

variables.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation.NUM_STREAMS
documentation: Number of random batches of images to send to the capsule

variables.name: vcap.testing.input_output_validation.NUM_BATCH_CASES
documentation: Number of batches of fuzzed inputs to perform on different images

classes.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.__init__
documentation:
---
param capsule_files: |-
  A dict of {"file_name": FILE_BYTES} of the files
  that were found and loaded in the capsule
param inference_mode: |-
  If True, the model will be loaded and the
  backends will start for it. If False, the capsule will never be
  able to run inference, but it will still have it's various readable
  attributes.
---

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.process_frame
documentation:
Find the Backend that has a BatchExecutor with the least amount of
images in the pipeline, and run with that Backend. In multi-GPU
scenarios, this is the logic that allows even usage across all GPUs.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.get_state
documentation: Get or create the StreamState for this stream_id and return it.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.clean_up
documentation: Dereference the StreamState that relates to this stream_id

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.close
documentation: De-initializes all backends.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.capability
documentation:
Returns a NodeDescription showing the difference between the input
and output NodeDescriptions for this capsule. This tells the caller
what service this capsule provides. For instance, a capsule that
classifies a person's gait would return the following:

```pycon
>>> NodeDescription(
...     size=NodeDescription.Size.ALL,
...     attributes={"Gait": ["walking", "running"]})
```

The fact that the capsule's input is a person is not shown in this
value because person detection is not something this capsule provides.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.default_options
documentation:
Return a dict of key->value where the value is the default value
for this capsules options

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.name
documentation:
The name of the capsule. This value uniquely defines the capsule and
cannot be shared by other capsules.

By convention, the capsule's name is prefixed by some short description
of the role it plays ("detector", "recognizer", etc) followed by the
kind of data it relates to ("person", "face", etc) and, if necessary,
some differentiating factor ("fast", "close_up", "retail", etc). For
example, a face detector that is optimized for quick inference would
be named "detector_face_fast".
These things are to be filled in by a subclassing capsule

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.version
documentation:
The version of the capsule.

When should you bump the version of a capsule? When:
    - You've changed the usage of existing capsule options
    - You've changed the model or algorithm
    - You've changed the input/output node descriptions

When shouldn't you bump the version of a capsule? When:
    - You only did code restructuring
    - You've updated the capsule to work with a newer API version
    - You've added (but not removed or changed previous) capsule
      options

In summary, the version is most useful for differentiating a capsule
from its previous versions.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.description
documentation: A human-readable description of what the capsule does.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.device_mapper
documentation:
A device mapper contains a single field, `filter_func`, which is a
function that takes in a list of all available device strings and
returns a list of device strings that are compatible with this capsule.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.backend_loader
documentation:
A function that creates a backend for this capsule.

```
param capsule_files

Provides access to all files in the capsule. The
keys are file names and the values are bytes.

param device

A string specifying the device that this backend should
use. For example, the first GPU device is specified as "GPU:0".
```

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.input_type
documentation:
Describes the types of DetectionNodes that this capsule takes in as
input.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.output_type
documentation:
Describes the types of DetectionNodes that this capsule produces as
output.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.options
documentation: A list of zero or more options that can be configured at runtime.

methods.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.stream_state
documentation:
(Optional) An instance of this object will be created for every new
video stream that a capsule is run on, and de-initialized when that
stream is deleted. It is intended to be overridden by capsules that
have stateful operations across a single stream.

class_variables.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.backends
documentation: A list of the backends the capsule has initialized

class_variables.name: vcap.capsule.BaseCapsule.backends_lock
documentation: Lock the list of backends whenever it's being accessed

An abstract base class that all capsules must subclass. Defines the
interface that capsules are expected to implement.

A class that subclasses from this class is expected to be defined in a
capsule.py file in a capsule.

functions.name: vcap.detection_node.rect_to_coords
documentation: Converts a rect in the [x1, y1, x2, y2] format to coordinates.

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.BoundingBox.size
documentation:
---
return: The width and height of the bounding box
---

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.BoundingBox.rect
documentation: Get the bounding box in 'rect' format, as (x1, y1, x2, y2)

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.BoundingBox.xywh
documentation:
Return the bounding box in 'xywh' format, as
(x1, y1, width, height)

classes.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode.__init__
documentation:
---
param attributes: |-
  A key-value store where the key is the type of
  attribute being described and the value is the attribute's value.
  For instance, a capsule that detects gender might add a "gender"
  key to this dict, with a value of either "masculine" or "feminine".
param children: |-
  Child DetectionNodes that are a "part" of the parent,
  for instance, a head DetectionNode might be a child of a person
  DetectionNode
param coords: |-
  A list of coordinates defining the detection as a
  polygon in-frame. Comes in the format `[[x,y], [x,y]...]`.
param encoding: "An array of float values that represent an encoding of\nthe detection.\
  \ This can be used to recognize specific instances of\na class. For instance, given\
  \ a picture of person\u2019s face, the\nencoding of that face and the encodings\
  \ of future faces can be\ncompared to find that person in the future."
param extra_data: "A dict of miscellaneous data. This data is provided\ndirectly to\
  \ clients without modification, so it\u2019s a good way to\npass extra information\
  \ from a capsule to other applications."
param name: |-
  The detection class name. This describes what the
  detection is. A detection of a person would have a name="person".
param track_id: |-
  If this object is tracked, this is the unique
  identifier for this detection node that ties it to other detection
  nodes in future and past frames within the same stream.
---

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode.scale
documentation:
Scales the detection coordinates of the tree by the given scales.

```
param scale_amount_x

The amount to scale x by

param scale_amount_y

The amount to scale y by
```

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode.offset
documentation:
Offsets the detection coordinates of the tree by the given offsets.

```
param offset_x

The amount to offset x by

param offset_y

The amount to offset y by
```

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode.all_attributes
documentation: Return all attributes including child attributes

methods.name: vcap.detection_node.DetectionNode._make_bbox
documentation:
---
return: |-
  Create a fully containing bounding box of the detection
  polygon
---

Capsules use DetectionNode objects to communicate results to other
capsules and the application itself. A DetectionNode contains information
on a detection in the current frame. Capsules that detect objects in a
frame create new DetectionNodes. Capsules that discover attributes about
detections add data to existing DetectionNodes.

classes.name: vcap.stream_state.BaseStreamState
documentation:
Filler class in case we want to add abstract methods in the future,
such as maybe requiring a method to clear/reset/copy StreamStates...

classes.name: vcap.loading.errors.CapsuleLoadError
documentation: Raised when there was an error loading a capsule.

classes.name: vcap.loading.errors.IncompatibleCapsuleError
documentation:
This is thrown when a capsule is of an incompatible API version for this
version of the library.

classes.name: vcap.loading.errors.InvalidCapsuleError
documentation:
This is thrown when a capsule has the correct API compatibility version,
but it still doesn't have the correct information on it.

classes.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription

methods.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.__init__
documentation:
---
param attributes: |-
  A dict whose key is the classification type and
  whose value is a list of possible attributes. A node that meets
  this description must have a classification for each classification
  type.
param detections: |-
  A list of acceptable detection class names. A node
  that meets this description must have a class name that is present
  in this list
param encoded: |-
  If true, the DetectionNode must be encoded to meet this
  description
param extra_data: |-
  A list of keys in a NodeDescription's extra_data. A
  DetectionNode that meets this description must have extra data for
  each name listed here.
param size: |-
  The number of DetectionNodes that this capsule either
  takes as input or provides as output
param tracked: If true, the DetectionNode is being tracked
---

methods.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.difference
documentation:
Outputs a NodeDescription that represents the change between this
NodeDescription and the given one. For instance, if the given
NodeDescription has been detected for people and this NodeDescription
has not, the resulting NodeDescription would look like this.

```pycon
>>> NodeDescription(size=NodeDescription.Size.ALL,
>>>                 detections=["person"])
```

This can be used to compare a capsule's input and output nodes to see
what operation the capsule provides.

methods.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.describes
documentation:
Check if a DetectionNode has the information described in this
NodeDescription.

class_variables.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.Size.NONE
documentation:
The capsule does not take any input. This is common for capsules
that detect objects in frame. These algorithms usually only need the
video frame.

class_variables.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.Size.SINGLE
documentation:
The capsule takes a single DetectionNode object. This is common for
capsules that find attributes for objects that have been detected by
other capsules.

class_variables.name: vcap.node_description.NodeDescription.Size.ALL
documentation:
The capsule takes all available DetectionNodes that fit the
capsule's input requirements. This is common for capsules that track
objects between video frames.

Capsules use NodeDescriptions to describe the kinds of DetectionNodes
they take in as input and produce as output.

A capsule may take a DetectionNode as input and produce zero or more
DetectionNodes as output. Capsules define what information inputted
DetectionNodes must have and what information outputted detection nodes
will have using NodeDescriptions.

For example, a capsule that encodes people and face detections would use
NodeDescriptions to define its inputs and outputs like so:

```pycon
>>> input_type = NodeDescription(
...     detections=["person", "face"])
>>> output_type = NodeDescription(
...     detections=["person", "face"],
...     encoded=True)
```

A capsule that uses a car's encoding to classify the color of a car would
look like this.

```pycon
>>> input_type = NodeDescription(
...     detections=["car"],
...     encoded=True)
>>> output_type = NodeDescription(
...     detections=["car"],
...     attributes={"color": ["blue", "yellow", "green"]},
...     encoded=True)
```

A capsule that detects dogs and takes no existing input would look like
this.

```pycon
>>> input_type = NodeDescription(size=NodeDescription.Size.NONE)
>>> output_type = NodeDescription(
>>>                 size=NodeDescription.Size.ALL,
>>>                 detections=["dog"])
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.vcap_packaging.package_capsule
documentation:
Packages an unpackaged capsule up into a zip and applies encryption.

```
param unpackaged_dir

The directory to package up

param output_file

The name and location to save the packaged capsule

param key

An AES key to encrypt the capsule with
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.vcap_packaging.unpackage_capsule
documentation:
Unpackagea capsule from the given bytes.

```
param path

The path to the capsule file

param key

The AES key to decrypt the capsule with, or None if the capsule
is not encrypted

return

None
```

variables.name: vcap.loading.vcap_packaging.CAPSULE_EXTENSION
documentation: The file extension that capsule files use.

variables.name: vcap.loading.vcap_packaging.CAPSULE_FILE_NAME
documentation: The name of the main file in the capsule.

variables.name: vcap.loading.vcap_packaging.META_FILE_NAME
documentation:
The name of the meta file that contains information such as the API 
compatibility version of this capsule

modules.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks
documentation:
Code which changes the way Python processes imports. Allows for capsules to
import Python modules and packages within the capsule itself.

functions.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks._package_fullname_to_path
documentation:
Converts a package's fullname to a file path that should be the package's
directory.

```
param fullname

The fullname of a package, like package_a.package_b

return

A derived filepath, like package_a/package_b
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks._module_fullname_to_path
documentation:
Converts a module's fullname to a file path that should be the module's
Python file.

```
param fullname

The fullname of a module, like package_a.my_module

return

A derived filepath, like package_a/my_module.py
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks._remove_capsule_name
documentation:
Remove "capsule_name" from capsule_name.some_module.some_module2
Since the files in the zip file won't have "capsule_name" in the paths

classes.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks.ZipFinder

methods.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks.ZipFinder.__init__
documentation:
---
param capsule_dir_path: |-
  The path to the directory where the development version of the
  capsule is stored. For example, if the capsule being loaded is in
  ../capsules/my_capsule_name.py

  Then capsule_dir_path would be
  ../capsules/my_capsule_name/

  The reason for this is so that debugging can work within capsules.
  When a capsule is executed, it is "compiled" to this path, so that
  debug information still works (along with breakpoints!)
param root_package_name: |-
  The name of the root package that this zip
  file provides
param zip_file: The ZipFile loaded in memory
---

An import finder that allows modules and packages inside a zip file to be
imported.

classes.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks.ZipModuleLoader

methods.name: vcap.loading.import_hacks.ZipModuleLoader.__init__
documentation:
---
param module_file_path: |-
  The path to where the python file would be
  in the filesystem if it was being run directly as opposed to in a
  .cap zip.
param zip_file: The ZipFile loaded in memory
---
Loads modules from a zip file.

functions.name: vcap.loading.capsule_loading.load_capsule_from_bytes
documentation:
Loads a capsule from the given bytes.

```
param data

The data of the capsule

param source_path

The path to the capsule's source code, if it's
available at runtime

param key

The AES key to decrypt the capsule with, or None if the capsule
is not encrypted

param inference_mode

If True, the backends for this capsule will be
started. If False, the capsule will never be able to run inference, but
it will still have it's various readable attributes.

return

The loaded capsule object
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.capsule_loading.load_capsule
documentation:
Load a capsule from the filesystem.

```
param path

The path to the capsule file

param source_path

The path to the capsule's source code, if it's
available at runtime

param key

The AES key to decrypt the capsule with, or None if the capsule
is not encrypted

param inference_mode

If True, the backends for this capsule will be
started. If False, the capsule will never be able to run inference, but
it will still have it's various readable attributes.
```

functions.name: vcap.loading.capsule_loading._validate_capsule
documentation:
This will try calling different attributes on a capsule to make sure
they are there. This is based on the API compatibility version.

:raises: InvalidCapsuleError

functions.name: vcap.loading.capsule_loading.capsule_module_name
documentation: Creates a unique module name for the given capsule bytes

variables.name: vcap.loading.capsule_loading.MINOR_COMPATIBLE_VERSION
documentation: The capsule version that this version of the library supports.

Encrypt the given source to dest.

The password to encrypt with

Data to encrypt

A file-like object to write results to

functions.name: vcap.loading.crypto_utils.decrypt

Decrypt the given bytes.

The password to decrypt with

Data to decrypt

return

The resulting bytes

functions.name: vcap.device_mapping.get_all_devices
documentation:
Returns a list of devices in the computer. Example:
['CPU:0', 'XLA_GPU:0', 'XLA_CPU:0', 'GPU:0', 'GPU:1', 'GPU:2', 'GPU:3']

methods.name: vcap.device_mapping.DeviceMapper.__init__
documentation:
The filter will take in a list of devices formatted as
["CPU:0", "CPU:1", "GPU:0", "GPU:1"], etc and output a filtered list of
devices.

methods.name: vcap.device_mapping.DeviceMapper.map_to_openvino_devices
documentation:
Intelligently load capsules onto available OpenVINO-compatible
devices.

Since support for OpenVINO devices is experimental, there is a
temporary environment variable being added to whitelist devices
specifically. This variable will be deprecated and removed after a
short testing period.

The device "CPU" is _always_ allowed and always loaded onto and cannot
be excluded.

Here are the cases:
['CPU:0', 'HDDL', ...] =>  ["MULTI:CPU,HDDL"]

['CPU:0'] => ["CPU"]
    Always load onto CPU.

classes.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend

methods.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend.send_to_batch
documentation:
Sends the given object to the batch_predict method for processing.
This call does not block. Instead, the result will be provided on the
returned Future. The batch_predict method must be overridden on the
backend this method is being called on.

```
param input_data

The input object to send to batch_predict

return

A Future where results will be stored
```

methods.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend.workload
documentation:
Returns a unit representing the amount of 'work' being processed
This value is comparable only by backends of the same capsule, and
is intended to give the scheduler the ability to pick the least busy
backend.

methods.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend.process_frame
documentation:
A method that does the pre-processing, inference, and postprocessing
work for a frame.

If the capsule uses an algorithm that benefits from batching,
this method may call `self.send_to_batch`, which will asynchronously
send work out for batching. Doing so requires that the
`batch_predict` method is overridden.

```
param frame

A numpy array representing a frame. It is of shape
(height, width, num_channels) and the frames come in BGR order.

param detection_node

The detection_node type as specified by the
input_type

param options

A dictionary of key (string) value pairs. The key is
the name of a capsule option, and the value is its configured value
at the time of processing. Capsule options are specified using the
options field in the Capsule class.

param state

This will be a StreamState object of the type specified
by the stream_state attribute on the Capsule class. If no
StreamState object was specified, a simple BaseStreamState object
will be passed in. The StreamState will be the same object for all
frames in the same video stream.
```

methods.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend.batch_predict
documentation:
This method takes in a batch as input and provides a list of result
objects of any type as output. What the result objects are will depend
on the algorithm being defined, but the number of prediction objects
returned \_must\_ match the number of video frames provided as input.

```
param input_data_list

A list of objects. Whatever the model requires
for each frame.
```

methods.name: vcap.backend.BaseBackend.close
documentation:
De-initializes the backend. This is called when the capsule is being
unloaded. This method should be overridden by any Backend that needs
to release resources or close other threads.

The backend will stop receiving frames before this method is
called, and will not receive frames again.

An object that provides low-level prediction functionality for batches
of frames.

modules.name: vcap.modifiers
documentation: Contains utility functions for doing data modification work in a capsule.

classes.name: vcap.modifiers.Crop

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Crop.from_detection
documentation: Uses the given detection area to create a crop.

A crop that may be applied to a frame.

Usage Example:
\>>> det_node = DetectionNode(...)
\>>> frame: np.ndarray
\>>> cropped = Crop.from_detection(det_node).pad_percent(top=10).apply(frame)

classes.name: vcap.modifiers.Clamp

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Clamp.__init__
documentation:
---
param frame: The frame to scale
param max_height: The maximum allowed height of the frame
param max_width: The maximum allowed width of the frame
---

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Clamp.apply
documentation:
---
return: A clamped version of the frame
---

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Clamp.scale_detection_nodes
documentation:
Scales up the given DetectionNode objects to fit the original frame
size.

Scales down a frame if its size is over a given amount, preserving the
original aspect ratio. It can then scale up any detections that have been
run on the smaller frame to fit the original frame size.

If the frame is under the given amount, nothing is done and the frame is
kept the same.

Usage Example:
\>>> clamp = Clamp(frame, 100, 100)
\>>> detection_nodes = some_detection_inference(clamp.apply())
\>>> clamp.scale_detection_nodes(detection_nodes)

classes.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.crop
documentation:
Crop the frame

```
param crop_width

Resulting width of the frame after cropping

param crop_height

Resulting height of the frame after cropping

param crop_type

Type of crop to perform

param top_left

[Optional] Point in input's coordinate space that will
```

serve as the top left point in the output. Only used if
CropPadType=CROP_START_POINT. Tuple in form (x, y)

methods.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.scale_and_offset_detection_nodes
documentation:
Scales up the given DetectionNode objects to fit the original frame
size.

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.WIDTH
documentation: Fit width exactly, maintain aspect ratio

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.HEIGHT
documentation: Fit height exactly, maintain aspect ratio

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.FIT_BOTH
documentation:
Fit both height and width within bounds, but only one will be exact.
Will end up smaller than (or same as) requested size.
Basically the opposite of FIT_ONE in implementation.

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.FIT_ONE
documentation:
Fit one of height or width within size, maintaining aspect ratio.
Will end up bigger than (or same as) requested size.
Basically the opposite of FIT_BOTH in implementation.
Usually followed by a crop.

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.EXACT
documentation:
Resize image exactly, ignoring aspect ratio. crop and pad attributes
will have no effect

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.ResizeType.NONE
documentation: Don't resize

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.CropPadType.RIGHT_BOTTOM
documentation: Crop/pad the larger index pixels

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.CropPadType.LEFT_TOP
documentation: Crop/pad the smaller index pixels

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.CropPadType.ALL
documentation:
Crop/pad evenly from both sides. Smaller index side will be one
greater than larger other if odd crop/pad)

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.CropPadType.CROP_START_POINT
documentation:
Crop to size with top left of result at top_left. Not available for
padding

class_variables.name: vcap.modifiers.Resize.CropPadType.NONE
documentation: Don't crop/pad

Resize frames to a specified size

Allows for maintaining aspect ratio
Allows for padding
Allows for cropping

Usage example:
\>>> # Resize up, then crop right/bottom side to be 480p
\>>> frame: np.ndarray
\>>> frame = (Resize(frame) ... .resize(1920, 1080, Resize.ResizeType.FIT_BOTH) ... .crop(640, 480, Resize.CropPadType.RIGHT_BOTTOM) ... .frame)

```pycon
>>> # Resize down, then pad all around to be back to 1080p
>>> frame: np.ndarray
>>> frame = (Resize(frame)
...          .resize(640, 480, Resize.ResizeType.FIT_BOTH)
...          .pad(1920, 1080, Resize.CropPadType.BOTH)
...          .frame)
```

classes.name: vcap.modifiers.SizeFilter
documentation:
Filters detection nodes based on if they're large or small enough.

Usage example:
\>>> nodes: List[DetectionNode]
\>>> filtered = (SizeFilter(nodes)
... .min_size(10, 10)
... .max_size(1000, 1000)
... .apply())

functions.name: vcap.options.check_option_values
documentation:
Checks the given option values against the capsule's available options to
make sure they are of the right name, type, and fit within the constraints.

classes.name: vcap.options.Option

methods.name: vcap.options.Option.check
documentation:
Checks the given value to see if it fits this option.

```
param value

The value to check

raises ValueError

If the value does not fit the option
```

A class that all options subclass. Added mostly for documentation an
type hinting purposes.

classes.name: vcap.options.FloatOption

methods.name: vcap.options.FloatOption.__init__
documentation:
---
param default: The default value of this option
param description: The description for this option
param max_val: |-
  The maximum allowed value for this option, inclusive,
  or None for no upper limit
param min_val: |-
  The minimum allowed value for this option, inclusive,
  or None for no lower limit
---

A capsule option that holds a floating point value with defined
boundaries.
